summary: Iteration 105.16 backend-only verification of the PyMeshFix-based /api/mesh/repair pipeline. All 6 review-request scenarios PASSED, plus the 4 prior-iteration regression tests still pass. Total: 10/10 tests green in ~8s.
critical_code_review_comments: mesh_repair.py:114-145 — MeshLab MeshSet is constructed per call (correct, avoids the FD leak called out in the docstring). Good.
mesh_repair.py:163 trimesh.load(..., force='mesh') — correctly handles multi-shell STLs as a single concatenated mesh before handing to PyMeshFix. Good.
mesh_repair.py:165 — faces cast to int32 ensures PyMeshFix native binding accepts the array; verts cast to float64 matches MeshFix C API expectations.
mesh_repair.py:185-187 trimesh.Trimesh(process=False) + fix_normals() — sound choice (avoids re-welding what PyMeshFix already finalized) while still emitting consistent normals for the STL writer.

mesh_repair.py:243-246 asyncio.wait_for around run_in_executor — note: cancellation of the awaitable does NOT actually kill the child process; on TimeoutError the worker keeps running until the filter returns. With max_workers=2 and a 90s ceiling this is acceptable, but two back-to-back 90s timeouts will starve the pool. Not blocking for this iteration; consider adding executor.shutdown(cancel_futures) + recreate on timeout in a follow-up.

mesh_repair.py:71-72 — docstring says workers are recycled after each repair, but ProcessPoolExecutor is constructed with the default max_tasks_per_child=None, so children are actually reused. Either implement max_tasks_per_child=1 or update the comment. Minor; the PyMeshFix path showed no FD leak in this run.
test_credentials: Bearer st_test_f9cb48812e2d4f29bd73d7bfef69aa1d (steve.shurts@gmail.com) — valid and accepted by /api/auth/me <2s and by /api/mesh/repair on both raw-bytes and multipart paths.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test suite at /app/tests/test_mesh_repair_and_auth.py now has 10 tests grouped in 6 classes. The new TestMeshRepairWatertight class covers the iter 105.16 PyMeshFix swap: (1) 12-tri cube round-trip → 12 tris watertight+oriented, (2) 10-tri holey cube → reconstructed to 12 watertight tris, (3) 22-tri broken sphere → output watertight (allowed to shrink in tri count — PyMeshFix produced a 6-tri sealed shell in this run, well under input 22), (4) multipart fallback path also returns the new X-Repair-Watertight/Winding-Consistent headers. All cases additionally re-parse the response bytes with trimesh and assert is_watertight AND is_volume — the latter is the exact gate that manifold-3d/three-bvh-csg use, so this is end-to-end proof that the original 'BOOLEAN CUT DROPPED — non-manifold host mesh' user report is fixed for clean fixtures. To re-verify after future changes, run: `cd /app && python -m pytest tests/test_mesh_repair_and_auth.py -v`. Whole suite finishes in ~8s.
rca of the issue: Original bug (iter 105.16): MeshLab `meshing_close_holes` filter only seals topologically-closed boundary loops, so isolated open edges and self-intersecting triangles on AI/photogrammetry STLs survived 'repair' and three-bvh-csg dropped the cube subtraction with 'host mesh is non-manifold'. Fix verified: pipeline now runs MeshLab prep (dedupe, merge, re-orient, T-vertex removal, drop ≤4-tri shards) then PyMeshFix 0.18 .repair(joincomp=True, remove_smallest_components=False) which guarantees a watertight 2-manifold output. The new X-Repair-Watertight=true / X-Repair-Winding-Consistent=true headers were observed on all 4 positive fixtures, and round-tripping the response through trimesh confirmed `is_watertight is True` AND `is_volume is True` — the exact precondition manifold-3d / three-bvh-csg require for boolean ops. Holey cube was correctly reconstructed from 10 tris back to 12. Broken sphere came out as a minimal sealed shell (output tris < input) which the test accepts per the success criterion in the review request.
